reasoning:
Looking at this problem through the lens of "critique" - I'm drawn to how critique in art reveals negative space and counterfactuals. What if the problem isn't "inconsistent saving" but "misaligned capture timing"?

Traditional retirement systems expect consistent small contributions. Irregular earners have *surges and droughts*. The insight: stop fighting the inconsistency. Design for aggressive surge capture, complete drought forgiveness.

```json
{
  "label": "Surplus Critique Accounts",
  "design_principles": "Embrace volatility as feature not bug. Capture surges ruthlessly, ignore droughts entirely. The 'critique' is continuous algorithmic assessment of what's truly surplus vs. needed.",
  "core_mechanism": "Algorithmic baseline detection identifies your 'survival threshold.' All income above threshold is auto-captured at 60-70% for retirement. Below-threshold months require $0—no guilt, no catch-up.",
  "how_it_works": "AI analyzes 24-month income history to set personalized baseline (survival needs + buffer). Each payment triggers surplus detection. Above baseline: immediate high-capture to retirement account with 24hr contestation window ('I have unusual expenses'—must specify, tracked for pattern abuse). Below baseline: $0 expected, $0 required. 5% of all captured surplus flows to mutualized 'drought coverage pool' that supplements retirements of chronic-drought members. Quarterly 'critique reports' show your capture efficiency and baseline accuracy. Annual human review for baseline recalibration.",
  "what_is_new": "Inverts the contribution model: instead of 'save consistently small amounts,' it's 'surrender surplus when it appears.' No catch-up anxiety. No guilt-based engagement. Treats irregular income as a poolable risk across similar earners.",
  "why_it_works": "Irregular earners often have windfall months they sub-optimize—money leaks to lifestyle inflation. Aggressive auto-capture in good months means they don't need discipline in bad months. Removes the impossible demand for consistency while capturing more total dollars than guilt-based voluntary systems.",
  "why_it_fails": "Baseline detection is genuinely hard—errors create either poverty (baseline too low) or resentment (baseline too high). Aggressive capture could discourage income growth ('why earn more if 70% vanishes?'). The contestation system invites gaming and requires expensive fraud detection.",
  "medium_term": "Gig platforms integrate surplus-capture as default. 'Baseline accuracy score' becomes a financial health metric. Irregular work loses some retirement penalty, attracting more workers.",
  "long_term_vision": "Retirement security decouples from income regularity. A freelancer with $200K in good years and $20K in bad years retires as securely as a salaried worker averaging $80K. The anxiety of inconsistent income—one of its deepest psychological costs—is eliminated. Work choices are made on merit, not fear."
}
```

solution.label: Surplus Critique Accounts
solution.design_principles: Embrace volatility as feature not bug. Capture surges ruthlessly, ignore droughts entirely. The 'critique' is continuous algorithmic assessment of what's truly surplus vs. needed.
solution.core_mechanism: Algorithmic baseline detection identifies your 'survival threshold.' All income above threshold is auto-captured at 60-70% for retirement. Below-threshold months require $0—no guilt, no catch-up.
solution.how_it_works: AI analyzes 24-month income history to set personalized baseline (survival needs + buffer). Each payment triggers surplus detection. Above baseline: immediate high-capture to retirement account with 24hr contestation window ('I have unusual expenses'—must specify, tracked for pattern abuse). Below baseline: $0 expected, $0 required. 5% of all captured surplus flows to mutualized 'drought coverage pool' that supplements retirements of chronic-drought members. Quarterly 'critique reports' show your capture efficiency and baseline accuracy. Annual human review for baseline recalibration.
solution.what_is_new: Inverts the contribution model: instead of 'save consistently small amounts,' it's 'surrender surplus when it appears.' No catch-up anxiety. No guilt-based engagement. Treats irregular income as a poolable risk across similar earners.
solution.why_it_works: Irregular earners often have windfall months they sub-optimize—money leaks to lifestyle inflation. Aggressive auto-capture in good months means they don't need discipline in bad months. Removes the impossible demand for consistency while capturing more total dollars than guilt-based voluntary systems.
solution.why_it_fails: Baseline detection is genuinely hard—errors create either poverty (baseline too low) or resentment (baseline too high). Aggressive capture could discourage income growth ('why earn more if 70% vanishes?'). The contestation system invites gaming and requires expensive fraud detection.
solution.medium_term: Gig platforms integrate surplus-capture as default. 'Baseline accuracy score' becomes a financial health metric. Irregular work loses some retirement penalty, attracting more workers.
solution.long_term_vision: Retirement security decouples from income regularity. A freelancer with $200K in good years and $20K in bad years retires as securely as a salaried worker averaging $80K. The anxiety of inconsistent income—one of its deepest psychological costs—is eliminated. Work choices are made on merit, not fear.
